title: BGISeq-500 library construction Protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"52dd6","text":"This single-tube library construction protocol is for degraded DNA using BGISeq-500 sequencing platform.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"c48qo","text":"Calculate the DNA input and prepare the library adapters.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: End-repair step following Carøe et al. 2017 with the addition of 1 μl "Reaction enhancer" consisting of 25% Poly-Ethylene Glycol (PEG4000); 2 μg/μL Bovine Serum Albumin (BSA) and 400 mM NaCl. Carøe C, Gopalakrishnan S, Vinner L, Mak SST, Sindin MHS, Samaniego JA, et al. Single-tube library preparation for degraded DNA. Methods in ecology and evolution; 2017; in press.

Ligation step following Carøe et al. 2017 by using 1 μl 10 μM BGISeq-500 adapters (Supplemental Table S4). Carøe C, Gopalakrishnan S, Vinner L, Mak SST, Sindin MHS, Samaniego JA, et al. Single-tube library preparation for degraded DNA. Methods in ecology and evolution; 2017; in press. Supplemental Table S4 (This study)

Fill-in step of the adapter to complete library building and purification using 1:5 volume of PB binding buffer (Qiagen) and using Monarch DNA Cleanup Columns (New England Biolabs, Massachusetts, USA). Carøe C, Gopalakrishnan S, Vinner L, Mak SST, Sindin MHS, Samaniego JA, et al. Single-tube library preparation for degraded DNA. Methods in ecology and evolution; 2017; in press.

To determine the number of cycles used in index PCR, performe a qPCR in a 20 µl reaction volume using 1:20 dilution of: purified library template 0.2 mM dNTPs (Invitrogen) 0.04 U/µl AmpliTaq Gold DNA polymerase (Applied Biosystems, Foster City, California, USA) 2.5 mM MgCl2 (Applied Biosystems) 1X GeneAmp® 10X PCR Buffer II (Applied Biosystems) 1 µl SYBR Green (Invitrogen, Carlsbad, California, USA) 0.2 µM forward and reverse primers mixture(CommonprimerBGI forward primer and one of the indexed reverse primers) (Supplemental Table S4) 13.48 µl AccuGene molecular biology water (Lonza)
qPCR cycling conditions: %s %s cycles temprature time 1 95 °C 10 min 40 95 °C 30 sec 60 °C 60 sec 72 °C 60 sec (using the Agilent MX3005 qPCR machine)

100 µl PCR reactions containing: 20 µl of purified library 0.2 mM dNTPs (Invitrogen) 0.1 U/µl AmpliTaq Gold DNA polymerase (Applied Biosystems) 2.5 mM MgCl2 (Applied Biosystems) 1X GeneAmp® 10X PCR Buffer II (Applied Biosystems) 0.4 mg/ml BSA (New England Biolabs Inc) 0.2 µM of each forward (CommonprimerBGI forward primer) indexed reverse primers (Supplemental Table S4) 51.2 µl AccuGene molecular biology water (Lonza, Basel, CH)
PCR cycling conditions: %s %s cycles temprature time initial denaturation 95 °C 12 min 13-21 95 °C 30 sec 60 °C 30 sec 72 °C 40 sec elongation step 72 °C 5 min
Post-PCR, purify libraries with QiaQuick columns (Qiagen).
Elute libraries with 30 µl buffer EB (Qiagen).

Use small amounts of aliquots after purification for concentration quantification and fragment size estimation with the High-Sensitivity DNA Assay kit 2100 expert High Sensitivity DNA Assay in Bioanalyzer 2100 (Agilent).

Extra purification using the AMpure XP system (Agentcourt, Beckman Counter, Indianapolis, USA) with 1.8X beads:library ratio, in order to remove any persisting primer dimers or other molecules with a fragment size of <100 bp.

Use small amounts of aliquots after purification for concentration quantification and fragment size estimation with the High-Sensitivity DNA Assay kit 2100 expert High Sensitivity DNA Assay in Bioanalyzer 2100 (Agilent).
Split each purified library to 2 aliquots (~12.5 µl each), and subject one of each aliquot to an extra purification to remove any residual primer dimers.
